questions.q: In which year was the Gandhi-Irwin Pact signed?
questions.explanation: The Gandhi-Irwin Pact was signed on 5 March 1931.

questions.q: The Gandhi-Irwin Pact was signed between Mahatma Gandhi and which British official?
questions.explanation: The pact was concluded between Gandhi and the Viceroy Lord Irwin, after whom it is named.

questions.q: What office did Lord Irwin hold when he signed the pact with Gandhi?
questions.explanation: Lord Irwin was the Viceroy of India at the time of the agreement.

questions.q: Which mass movement did the Gandhi-Irwin Pact suspend?
questions.explanation: The pact provided for the suspension of the Civil Disobedience Movement launched in 1930.

questions.q: The Gandhi-Irwin Pact paved the way for Gandhi's participation in which event?
questions.explanation: Following the pact, Gandhi attended the Second Round Table Conference in London in 1931.

questions.q: On which substance did the pact grant a key concession to coastal Indians?
questions.explanation: The pact allowed people near the coast to make salt for their own use, a concession on the salt law.

questions.q: The Gandhi-Irwin Pact is sometimes described as which kind of agreement?
questions.explanation: It was essentially a truce that paused the confrontation between the Congress and the colonial government.

questions.q: One major provision of the pact concerned the fate of which group of people?
questions.explanation: The government agreed to release political prisoners held for nonviolent offences during the movement.

questions.q: In which city was the Gandhi-Irwin Pact signed?
questions.explanation: The pact was negotiated and signed in Delhi in early 1931.

questions.q: The Gandhi-Irwin Pact is also commonly known by which other name?
questions.explanation: Because it was concluded in Delhi, the agreement is also referred to as the Delhi Pact.

questions.q: On what exact date was the Gandhi-Irwin Pact signed?
questions.explanation: The pact was signed on 5 March 1931 in Delhi.

questions.q: What was the full title of the Viceroy Lord Irwin who signed the pact?
questions.explanation: Lord Irwin was Edward Frederick Lindley Wood, later the Earl of Halifax.

questions.q: The Civil Disobedience Movement suspended by the pact had begun with which earlier event?
questions.explanation: The Civil Disobedience Movement was launched by Gandhi's Dandi Salt March of 1930.

questions.q: Under the pact, the government agreed to withdraw which type of measures issued during the movement?
questions.explanation: The government agreed to withdraw the emergency ordinances it had promulgated to suppress the movement.

questions.q: Which Congress demand did the pact NOT concede, leaving it unresolved?
options: Commutation of the death sentences of Bhagat Singh and his comrades
Permission to make salt near the coast
questions.explanation: Gandhi pressed for commutation of the sentences of Bhagat Singh, Rajguru and Sukhdev, but Irwin did not concede it.

questions.q: At which Congress session, held shortly after the pact, was it endorsed by the party?
questions.explanation: The Congress endorsed the Gandhi-Irwin Pact at its Karachi session in March 1931.

questions.q: Which prominent British statesman famously expressed disgust at Gandhi negotiating 'on equal terms' with the Viceroy?
questions.explanation: Winston Churchill expressed contempt at the sight of Gandhi parleying on equal terms with the King-Emperor's representative.

questions.q: The pact allowed the peaceful picketing of which kind of goods to continue?
questions.explanation: The pact permitted peaceful and non-coercive picketing of foreign cloth and liquor shops.

questions.q: Which Round Table Conference had taken place before the pact, with the Congress largely absent?
options: The Second Round Table Conference of 1931
The First Round Table Conference of 1930-31
The Third Round Table Conference of 1932
questions.explanation: The First Round Table Conference of 1930-31 was held while Congress leaders were in jail, so the Congress did not attend.

questions.q: After the Karachi session, in what capacity did Gandhi go to the Second Round Table Conference?
options: As an observer for the Muslim League
As a delegate of the princely states
As a member of the Viceroy's council
As the sole representative of the Congress
questions.explanation: Gandhi attended the Second Round Table Conference as the sole representative of the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: Many in the Congress were dismayed that the pact came just weeks before which executions in March 1931?
options: The hangings of Bhagat Singh, Rajguru and Sukhdev
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh, Rajguru and Sukhdev were hanged on 23 March 1931, soon after the pact was signed.

questions.q: The pact is regarded as significant because it was negotiated between the colonial government and which body as an equal party?
questions.explanation: It marked the British negotiating with the Congress as a roughly equal party, a notable departure for the colonial government.

questions.q: Lord Irwin, the Viceroy who signed the pact, later took which title in the British peerage?
questions.explanation: Edward Wood, Lord Irwin, later became the Earl of Halifax and served as Britain's Foreign Secretary.

questions.q: Negotiations leading to the pact were partly facilitated by intermediaries including which liberal leader?
questions.explanation: Liberal leaders such as Tej Bahadur Sapru and M. R. Jayakar helped facilitate the talks between Gandhi and Irwin.

questions.q: The pact did not grant a Congress request for a public inquiry into police conduct, instead leaving it as what?
options: A demand Gandhi did not press to a breach
A clause added in a secret annex
questions.explanation: Gandhi sought an inquiry into police excesses but did not let its refusal break the agreement, accepting Irwin's position.

questions.q: Which earlier 1929 declaration by the Congress formed the backdrop against which the pact was seen by critics as a compromise?
questions.explanation: Having declared Purna Swaraj (complete independence) in 1929, some felt the pact's limited gains fell short of that goal.

questions.q: Under the pact, the right to make salt was conceded chiefly for whom?
options: Villagers in areas where salt could be collected locally
questions.explanation: The concession allowed people in salt-producing coastal areas to collect or make salt for personal use and village sale.

questions.q: After the Second Round Table Conference failed to satisfy Congress, what happened to the truce established by the pact?
options: It was converted into a constitution
Civil Disobedience was resumed in early 1932
questions.explanation: When talks broke down, the Congress relaunched Civil Disobedience in 1932 and the truce effectively ended.

questions.q: Which future British Prime Minister was Secretary of State for India during the period of the pact and the Round Table talks?
questions.explanation: William Wedgwood Benn served as Secretary of State for India under the Labour government at the time of the pact.

questions.q: Historians often note that, in concrete gains, the Gandhi-Irwin Pact secured Indian concessions that were chiefly described as what?
questions.explanation: Scholars generally regard the pact's substantive concessions as limited, while its symbolic value of equal negotiation was significant.
